name: Capon of the North
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the capon into pieces as for fricassee.
2. Dip each piece into lemon juice, then into seasoned flour (salt and pepper).
3. Brown the pieces in butter.
4. Add diced bacon and small yellow onions; sprinkle with additional flour and brown again.
5. Flame the capon with Irish whisky.
6. Sprinkle with salt, pepper, garlic, and allspice.
7. Cover with red wine and simmer gently for 60 minutes or until cooked through.
8. To make the sauce, prepare a broth using the neck, feet, and giblets from the bird.
9. Strain off the liquor and add to the broth; reduce by rapid boiling until halved.
10. Thicken the sauce with raw egg yolks beaten with cream; reheat without boiling after adding the eggs.
11. Serve the capon in a dish garnished with pearl onions and mushrooms (cooked separately in water and lemon juice).
12. Pour part of the sauce over the capon and serve the remainder separately.
Makes 8 to 10 servings.
